channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Dirty romance novels are shedding their stigma thanks to TikTok
item.description: By now everyone is familiar with #BookTok—but have you stumbled across its spicier cousin #SmutTok? “Smut” refers to books that include one or more sexually explicit scenes and surprisingly (or perhaps unsurprisingly) the erotic category has found a growing fan base on TikTok.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: How a TikTok trend to help moms became a money-grab opportunity
item.description: A sparkly silver purse, tucked between a bottle of baby lotion and Infants’ Tylenol at a retailer, sparked a recent viral trend. Denaesha Gonzalez noticed the purse (with the price tag still dangling) in the baby product aisle while out shopping.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Samsung's Galaxy S24 FE Exynos chip beats the iPhone 16 in one key graphics test
item.description: The more affordable Galaxy S24 FE will hold its own against the flagships as far as gaming performance goes.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: 3 things to get right with data management for gen AI projects
item.description: According to Kari Briski, VP of AI models, software, and services at Nvidia, successfully implementing gen AI hinges on effective data management and evaluating how different models work together to serve a specific use case.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft releases official OpenAI library for .NET
item.description: Microsoft has released its official OpenAI library for .NET, with the goal of ensuring a smooth, reliable integration experience for developers working with OpenAI and Azure OpenAI services in .NET applications. Announced October 1, the now-stable library follows a beta release published in June.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Huawei Connect 2024: Reimagining a future powered by AI and industrial digitalisation
item.description: Huawei has conjured a grand vision: accelerate the integration of artificial intelligence (AI) into industries globally to intelligently drive transformation within each sector.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Details from New Mexico's lawsuit against Snap show site failed to act on reports of sextortion
item.description: Snapchat failed to act on “rampant” reports of child grooming, sextortion and other dangers to minors on its platform, according to a newly unredacted complaint against the company filed by New Mexico's attorney general. Attorney General Raúl Torrez filed the original complaint on Sept.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Mastering Social Media Etiquette: A Guide to Posting Playbook
item.description: Navigating social media rules, Partiful etiquette, and digital communication in the online world
item.dc:creator: Steffi Cao, Fast Company

item.title: Africa's Fintech Industry on the Rise: Overcoming Regulatory Hurdles to Reach $230 Billion Revenue
item.description: Africa's fintech industry poised for growth, but regulatory challenges must be addressed to reach $230 billion revenue by 2025.
item.dc:creator: Olugbenga Agboola, Fast Company

item.title: FAA confirms SpaceX Falcon 9 is grounded because of Crew-9 launch issue
item.description: The agency is investigating why the rocket's second stage missed its deorbit landing target zone after this weekend's Crew-9 launch.
item.dc:creator: Richard Tribou, The Mercury News

item.title: Two good Visual Studio Code alternatives
item.description: The conductor of my choir famously tells us singers, “I only want everything all the time.” Well, as a developer, my mantra for code editors and IDEs (integrated development environments) is exactly that: I only want everything all the time.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Arkansas sues YouTube over claims that the site is fueling a mental health crisis
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — Arkansas sued YouTube and parent company Alphabet on Monday, saying the video-sharing platform is made deliberately addictive and fueling a mental health crisis among youth in the state.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The iPhone 16 Pro Max is a bigger upgrade than I was expecting
item.description: My iPhone 13 Pro Max was working just fine when I decided to upgrade to the latest model. The 13 Pro Max still feels fast, takes excellent photos, and easily lasts all day on a full charge even at 88% battery health.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: Trump says he's spoken to Musk about getting Starlink for areas damaged by Helene
item.description: Former President Trump on Monday said he’s spoken with Elon Musk about providing Starlink internet service for those without power in states ravaged by Hurricane Helene. Trump visited Valdosta, Ga., where he toured storm damage and expressed support for those dealing with the aftermath.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Fortnite maker accuses Samsung, Google of colluding to block app store competitors
item.description: Fortnite maker Epic Games filed a lawsuit Monday accusing Google and Samsung, the world’s largest Android maker, of conspiring to block third-party competitors of the Google Play Store.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Making use of command history on Linux
item.description: Command history on Linux allows you to review and repeat previous commands with little effort. It is built into the bash shell (included in the shell executable).
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Verizon, AT&T, T-Mobile service outages reported by users
item.description: People in multiple cities posted about issues they were having, with many saying their phones were in "SOS" mode.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: X's value has fallen 79% since Musk takeover, Fidelity estimates
item.description: Fidelity, which originally invested $19.66 million, now values its stake in the company at $4.19 million.
item.dc:creator: Katie Smith, NewsNation

item.title: How to succeed with Kubernetes
item.description: Kubernetes, the open source orchestration platform for automating the deployment and management of containerized workloads and services, continues to gain popularity.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Montana man gets 6 months in prison for cloning giant sheep and breeding it
item.description: An 81-year-old Montana has been sentenced to six months in federal prison and ordered to pay $24,000 in penalties for using tissue and testicles from large sheep hunted in Central Asia and the U.S. to illegally create hybrid sheep for captive trophy hunts in Texas and Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: Amy Beth Hanson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Newsom vetoes bill for stricter AI regulations
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom (D) on Sunday vetoed a landmark artificial intelligence (AI) bill that would have created new safety rules for the emerging tech, handing much of Silicon Valley a major win.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Android's latest nightmare: millions of devices infected by sneaky malware
item.description: There have been cases when widely downloaded apps from the Play Store are infected with malware. Kurt the CyberGuy tackles the issue of a new Trojan malware.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Brazil judge makes new requests to allow X to be reinstated from suspension
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes on Friday added conditions for Elon Musk’s X to have its service reestablished in the country, one day after the social media platform said it had complied with all the judge’s demands, including naming a legal representative.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump threatens to prosecute Google over search results
item.description: Former President Trump on Friday threatened to prosecute Google if elected in November, alleging the search engine was “only” showing "bad” stories about him and "good” articles about Vice President Harris.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Zscaler Brings Clarity to Zero Trust and AI Conversation - 2023
item.description: Zscaler discusses the importance of zero trust architecture, AI-powered security solutions, and how they bring these two concepts together to enhance cybersecurity postures.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Amazon's $4 billion partnership with AI startup Anthropic gets UK competition clearance
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain's competition watchdog said Friday that it's clearing Amazon's partnership with artificial intelligence company Anthropic because the $4 billion deal didn't qualify for further scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Kristen Bell is a Meta AI voice after criticizing company in post
item.description: After posting an "opt out" declaration on Instagram, the actress has now signed with Meta to use her voice in its AI chatbot.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: X requests it be reinstated in Brazil after complying with judge's orders, sources say
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — In the high-stakes showdown between the world’s richest man and a Brazilian Supreme Court justice, Elon Musk blinked. Musk’s social media site X has complied with Alexandre de Moraes’ orders and requested its service be reestablished in the country, two sources said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Police are probing apparent cyber vandalism on Wi-Fi networks at UK train stations
item.description: LONDON (AP) — U.K. transport officials and police said Thursday they are investigating a “cyber-security incident” after users of public Wi-Fi networks at the country’s biggest railway stations reported being shown anti-Muslim messages.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NTSB engineer says carbon fiber hull from submersible showed signs of flaws
item.description: The carbon fiber hull of the experimental submersible that imploded en route to the wreckage of the Titanic had imperfections dating to the manufacturing process and behaved differently after a loud bang was heard on one of the dives the year before the tragedy, an engineer with the National Transpo
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and DAVID SHARP, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: X releases its first transparency report since Elon Musk's takeover
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Social media platform X on Wednesday published its first transparency report since the company was purchased by Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: CrowdStrike executive apologizes to Congress for July global tech outage
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An executive at cybersecurity company CrowdStrike apologized in testimony to Congress for sparking a global technology outage over the summer. “We let our customers down,” said Adam Meyers, who leads CrowdStrike's threat intelligence division, in a hearing before a U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: F5, NetApp team to streamline AI app deployments
item.description: F5 and NetApp announced plans to combine their technologies to help enterprise customers securely deploy AI applications across multicloud environments.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Video game actors' union calls for strike against 'League of Legends'
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — “League of Legends” is caught in the middle of a dispute between Hollywood’s actors union and an audio company that provides voiceover services for the blockbuster online multiplayer game.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, Fox 5
